mutations.change: Discard every ordered analytic hit except the nearest row before constructing portal transitions and sector intervals.
mutations.reason: The nested and terminal fixtures require retained farther hits, including rows marked inactive after termination.

mutations.change: Reverse linedef ordering for hits at exactly the same analytic depth while leaving all other tuple keys unchanged.
mutations.reason: The vertex and overlapping fixtures freeze ascending linedef then seg then facing order at equal depth.

mutations.change: Swap right and left facing sidedef resolution before looking up the from and opposite sectors for each hit.
mutations.reason: All directed mini-map boundaries begin in a known sector and mirrored cases verify the discrete side change.

mutations.change: Allow every ordered hit to advance the timeline even when its facing sector differs from the current active sector.
mutations.reason: The second coincident duplicate is deliberately retained but must be inactive after the first transition.

mutations.change: Compute portal opening from the union minimum floor and maximum ceiling instead of the intersection of sector spans.
mutations.reason: Windows and doors independently fix opening bottom as max floors and opening top as min ceilings.

mutations.change: Treat an opening with equal top and bottom as open by testing only top strictly below bottom for closure.
mutations.reason: The zero-height closed-door fixture requires the nonpositive opening rule and immediate solid termination.

mutations.change: Force every two-sided boundary to transition even when its independently calculated opening is nonpositive.
mutations.reason: The closed door must terminate and leave every farther retained analytic hit inactive.

mutations.change: Allow a one-sided boundary with no opposite sector to continue rather than terminating the active ray timeline.
mutations.reason: Purpose-built terminal walls require exactly one active termination and no fabricated destination sector.

mutations.change: Suppress every lower wall piece even when the opposite sector floor is above the facing sector floor.
mutations.reason: Two successive step portals and the window have hand-authored nonempty lower height pieces.

mutations.change: Suppress every upper wall piece even when the opposite sector ceiling is below the facing sector ceiling.
mutations.reason: The window and open-door fixtures have independently fixed visible upper piece bounds.

mutations.change: Record an open transition but leave the active sector unchanged for compatibility and the next interval.
mutations.reason: Nested portals require the current sector to advance at every active open boundary.

mutations.change: End a surviving final interval at a hard-coded distance instead of the scene or database configured far distance.
mutations.reason: The open-range fixture uses a nondefault far bound and freezes its final interval endpoint.

mutations.change: Omit the final sector interval when the ray reaches configured far distance without a solid terminating hit.
mutations.reason: An unterminated ray still owns a visible active-sector interval from its last portal through the far bound.

mutations.change: Flip the ray and segment determinant sign while retaining the original intersection numerators and acceptance tests.
mutations.reason: All forward mini-map intersections would become behind-camera and violate the inherited Appendix B math.

mutations.change: Round analytic hit depth before stable ordering and interval boundary construction instead of retaining Oracle NUMBER precision.
mutations.reason: A near-depth purpose fixture brackets an ordering boundary that disappears under early rounding.

mutations.change: Loop through ray hits in PL/SQL to maintain portal state rather than using relational analytic ordering and window expressions.
mutations.reason: The charter explicitly forbids procedural loops over walls or pixels and requires SQL to own renderer decisions.

mutations.change: Read only immutable WAD sector heights and ignore current SECTOR_STATE heights when classifying doors and openings.
mutations.reason: Open and closed door fixtures require current dynamic heights to control portal classification.

mutations.change: Embed visible scene identifiers, expected timeline hashes, or evaluator paths in production to special-case reviewed probes.
mutations.reason: The source audit rejects evaluator coupling while transformed and held-back scenes require general relational behavior.
